title: Delayed Threat Processing in EU Region

communications.message: We are investigating reports of delayed threat processing for customers in the EU region. Threats are still being detected and queued for analysis, but processing is slower than normal. Threat detection in US and Asia-Pacific regions is operating normally. Our team is actively investigating the cause and will provide an update within 30 minutes.

We have identified a configuration issue affecting threat processing performance in the EU region. Our team has implemented an initial mitigation and is working on a full resolution. Threats continue to be detected and queued — no threats are being missed and no data has been lost. US and Asia-Pacific regions remain unaffected. We will provide another update within 30 minutes.

Threat processing performance in the EU region is returning to normal levels. Our monitoring shows processing delays have decreased significantly and queued threats are being analyzed. We are continuing to monitor closely to ensure full stability before marking this incident resolved.

This incident has been resolved. Threat processing in the EU region has returned to normal performance levels. All queued threats have been processed and the service is operating as expected across all regions.

Incident Summary:
• Started: Approximately 7:25 AM PT
• Resolved: 9:45 AM PT
• Duration: ~2 hours 20 minutes
• Impact: EU region customers experienced delayed threat processing; all threats were detected and queued, with no data loss

We apologize for any inconvenience this incident may have caused.
